blockers.description: The prompt looks for "schools located in a street named after a relevant historic figure", but it is unclear which exact values to look for.
example_questions: Which exact values should I look for when looking for relevant historic figures?
Which exact names do you refer by relevant historic figures?
Which exact names should I use when looking for relevant historic figures?
blockers.resolution: When looking for schools located in a street named after a relevant historic figure, only look for streets containing "Martin Luther King Junior", "Jack London", "Grant", "Lafayette", or "Adams".

blockers.description: The prompt requires to use the average enrollment per school in Mexico, which can not be known unless searched for
example_questions: What is the average enrollment per school in Mexico?
What value should I use as the average enrollment per school in Mexico?
Could you provide the average enrollment per school in Mexico?
blockers.resolution: The average enrollment per school in Mexico is 110 students

blockers.description: The business information notes that schools with higher SAT participation may appear stronger due to volume rather than performance quality, creating ambiguity in how participation should influence performance evaluation.
example_questions: What exact formula should I use when calculating the best-performing SAT scores?
How can I calculate the best-performing SAT scores?
Which equation should I use when calculating the best-performing SAT scores?
blockers.resolution: SAT performance must be computed as the average of all three SAT disciplines multiplied by NumGE1500.

blockers.description: Multiple columns show Sat-lite values and are named AvGScrWrite*, making it imposible to know which one to use
example_questions: Which columns should be treated as the authoritative SAT performance table?
Which are the columns I should use to find the SAT scores?
Which table and column should be used to calculate the SAT performance?
blockers.resolution: The satscores table is source for SAT performance, using the columns AvgScrMath2, AvgScrRead2, AvgScrWrite2.
